value.0: Favored by rogues and rangers, this enchanted suit of elven leather armor provides added protection and increased stealth.
A suit of green and brown leather armor.
Favored by rogues and rangers, this enchanted suit of elven leather armor provides added protection and increased stealth.
A suit of green and brown leather armor.

Platemail is composed of metal plates held together by sections of chainmail.  Dwarven made platemail offers superb protection while being lighter and quiter.  Large races will find it impossible to fit inside of this armor.
Platemail is composed of metal plates held together by sections of chainmail.  It offers excellent protection to all types of physical attacks but is very heavy and noisy.
Favored by rogues and rangers, this enchanted suit of elven leather armor provides added protection and increased stealth.
A suit of green and brown leather armor.
Suits of hardened leather are created by boiling the leather in water, as the leather cools it shrinks and hardens.  Hardened leather armor is often studded for added protection.
A suit of hardened leather armor.
Suits of armor such as this are made using normal cotton, wool, and sometimes silk.  It offers very little protection, but it is extremely lightweight and is completely silent.
This leather armor has steel studs embedded in its surface.
A suit of studded leather armor.

A chain shirt offers excellent defense versus slashing weapons but is practically useless in combat against blunt weapons.  It weighs considerably less than a full suit of chainmail and although it offers great mobility it is not completely silent.
Full suits of chainmail offer excellent protection against slashing weapons but are susceptible to crushing damage.  Though very flexible, movement will cause some noise.

Full suits of chainmail offer excellent protection against slashing weapons but are susceptible to crushing damage.  Elven smiths have imbued this chainmail with magics to make it  extremely lightweight and silent as well as magic resistant.  Larger races will find it impossible to fit inside of this armor.

Ringmail is essentially the same as chainmail armor, larger links and a looser mesh make it lighter than chainmail with similar protection.  Ringmail however can be more easily pierced by missile weapons and long thin blades.

Scalemail is made by overlapping small, scale shaped pieces of metal much like a snake's skin.  It protects well against slashing and blunt weapons, but piercing weapons are able to slip between the scales easily.  It is heavy in comparison to it's protection and the scales scrape together while moving.

Having explored the northlands extensively, a druid named Aawill commissioned the creation of these comfortable boots. Knowing he was heading for the sometimes-dangerous domain of the Neverwinter Woods, Aawill was looking for something helpful in combat, to lighten his need for armor. Since becoming the Archdruid for the Neverwinter Wood's Druid Grove, Aawill has commissioned several, more powerful versions of his original boots.

Named for the creatures most closely associated with beauty, Nymph Cloaks are popular fodder for tavern tales among the common folk. The story usually involves some hapless noble couple spending a fortune on one or more such items so they can stand to look at one another. It always gets a big laugh among a copper-ale crowd, though most professional bards consider such pandering a tool of the unskilled.

In the darkness that precedes the light many brave warriors will fall to the legions of the eye.  Haunted by dreams of the deaths of her people Rudash forged these robes to allow the powerful magic users of Galadhrim the time to bring there terrible spells to bear.
Being made from adamantite this shield offers better protection than it's normal cousins.
This shield made from Adamantite is stronger than it's counterpart making it better suited for deflecting attacks.
Being Made from Adamantite this shield offers better deflection then normal shields.

It was the Year of Slaughter, 1090 DR, and countless victims lay slain at the Battle of the Bones. Today the site contains layers of dry white bone, but at the time it was all blood and bile, oozing from each shattered corpse and saturating the fallen equipment. No amount of polishing has been able to restore any weapon retrieved from that dread place, or even begin to remove the taint acquired there.
Frost Reaver does both acidic and Frost damage to it's target hence how it got it's name....
Great Sword made out of Adamantite.
Bastard Sword made out of adamantite.
This Great Axe is made of Adamantite

Tormented by voices that incessantly attacked his mind, young Resino of Beregost begged his local church to kill him. Understanding his pain but unable to identify any specific demonic influence, the church tried crafting rings that they hoped would clear his thoughts. Unfortunately, while the rings enhanced his senses they did nothing for the voices, and Resino eventually took his own life.

Confounded by the inability to wear more than two magical rings at once, and desiring the powers of several of his own rings, Jagen Marsk, a Turmish noble, researched the combining of powers in magical rings. While what he was really hoping for was to combine invisibility with teleportation, he eventually had to settle with this ring, that provided a measure of magical protection, with a handy light source. He now sells these rings to finance his ever-growing obsession with more powerful combinations.
This amulet grants it's wearer increased endurance.

Although it appears to be a simple pearl on a light chain, a Periapt of Wisdom is a powerful item. These items improve perception and insight, and are often used as meditative tools by high-ranking clergy of a faith.

This ring grants bonus spells to a wizard.

This is the 3rd of 4 rings

Upon cursory examination, this ring appears to be made of ivory. Study reveals that the thick band is actually made of well-tooled elf bone. It bears the repeating angular geometric patterns that the long-dead craftsmen of Myth Lharast were known for. Considered plain by modern elven standards, the ring was a gift to Kiran-Hai, Circle Singer of Angharradh and Ward Mistress of the Arboreal Tombs in Myth Lahl. The name of its creator has been lost. It is believed to have been stolen from the Arboreal Tombs some time after Myth Lharast fell.
The mighty Wizards of Galadhrim are almost invunerable with the assitance of this belt
